mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound, sustained near-zero policy rates compress the equity risk premium discount factor, mechanically supporting forward S&P 500 returns as investors rotate from cash/bonds into equities seeking yield. This is an always-on carry effect independent of any threshold crossing.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX underperforms baseline unconditional mean return during ZIRP episodes with p>0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE, currently elevated at 10.2%, begins declining meaningfully from a cyclical peak, it signals the early stage of a labor market recovery that precedes broad economic reflation. A decline of at least 0.5 percentage points over 3 months triggers a positive equity response over the following quarter as forward earnings expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.5pp over trailing 3 months from a local peak
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=10.2 elevated, consistent with recessionary peak
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following triggered UNRATE declines are not significantly greater than unconditional baseline (t-test p>0.05), or hit rate for positive returns falls below 55% across triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Joint improvement in PAYEMS (rising employment) and INDPRO (rising industrial output) confirms a broad-based real-economy recovery that is not decomposable from either series alone; simultaneous positive month-over-month moves in both signal reduced recession risk and drive S&P 500 gains over the next month, whereas divergence between the two produces no reliable signal.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM change > 0 AND INDPRO MoM change > 0 simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from episodes where only one series improves (ANOVA p>0.10), invalidating the claimed interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-rate regime where FEDFUNDS is near 0.09%, rising CPIAUCSL erodes real interest rates further, boosting real equity valuations via lower discount rates; this positive inflation-equity linkage reverses in normal-rate regimes where rising CPI would instead signal future tightening and compress valuations. The mechanism is thus conditional on the prevailing rate regime.
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy regime with rising inflation
failure_condition: Correlation between CPIAUCSL MoM changes and forward 60d SPX returns is negative or insignificant (p>0.05) while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, contradicting the regime-conditional sign flip.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) serve as a leading indicator of forward-looking consumer and business confidence; sustained month-over-month increases in HOUST precede broader demand expansion and are associated with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month, functioning as an always-on trend signal without any threshold gating.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST trend and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 across any rolling 3-year window, indicating no persistent predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) above a 2% month-over-month growth threshold signals an unexpectedly strong consumer demand surge that is rapidly priced into equities within days, producing short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns as analysts revise near-term earnings estimates upward.
failure_condition: Following triggered RSAFS surges, SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) or hit rate is below 55% across triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects durable expansion in aggregate output and corporate revenue capacity, supporting persistent upward drift in S&P 500 prices over a quarterly horizon as forward earnings estimates track realized GDP momentum.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing GDPC1 growth rate and forward 60d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window despite continued positive GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of high unemployment slack (UNRATE well above equilibrium) and near-zero FEDFUNDS reflects a policy commitment to accommodative conditions that will persist until labor markets heal; this dual condition amplifies the equity-supportive effect of low rates beyond what either series predicts alone, driving stronger forward returns than a simple low-rate signal.
regime_hint: crisis-era accommodative policy with high labor slack
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns during joint-trigger periods are not statistically different from periods where only the low-rate condition holds (p>0.10), disproving the claimed interaction amplification.
